provenance.source_corpus: Can Mulch and Fertilizer Alone Rehabilitate Surface-disturbed Subarctic Peatlands?

source_spans.text: Rock phosphate fertilization and straw mulch did not increase the recolonization of Sphagnum nor of other bryophytes, lichens or vascular plants.
Sphagnum remained almost absent and bare peat was colonized mostly by lichens and bryophytes typical of disturbed peat surfaces.

nodes.name: Colonization of bare peat by lichens

source_spans.text: Sphagnum remained almost absent and bare peat was colonized mostly by lichens and bryophytes typical of disturbed peat surfaces.

nodes.name: Colonization of bare peat by bryophytes

source_spans.text: Sphagnum remained almost absent and bare peat was colonized mostly by lichens and bryophytes typical of disturbed peat surfaces.
Cut hummocks on winter road clearances appear to be especially recalcitrant to recolonization by Sphagnum and typical assemblages of peatlands plants.
Rock phosphate fertilization and straw mulch did not increase the recolonization of Sphagnum nor of other bryophytes, lichens or vascular plants.
